title: V-Model Quality Bar — per-artifact checklist container
description: Validates the shape of <type>.quality-bar.json files. Each file is the machine-readable form of the 5th section ("Quality Bar") of the corresponding docs/guide/artifacts/<type>.html page. HTML is authoritative for content; the JSON is an extracted index consumed by authoring skills, review skills, and the Quality Bar runner tool (TARGET_ARCHITECTURE §6, §10). Schema validates container shape only: group structure, item IDs, applies_at scope levels, and optional structured references. Item prompt wording is reviewed against the HTML, not by this schema.

artifact_type.description: Which of the six canonical spec artifact types this Quality Bar applies to. Matches the filename stem of the HTML source (e.g., "adr" for docs/guide/artifacts/adr.html).

version.description: Monotonic integer version of the extracted checklist. Bumped when group or item content is revised to match an updated HTML page. Git carries history; no multi-version files on disk.

extracted_from.description: Relative path to the authoritative HTML page. Extraction provenance — lets a future re-extraction tool find the source without convention assumptions.

extracted_at.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the extraction pass. Lets consumers detect staleness relative to HTML mtime or recent commits.

groups.description: Ordered list of checklist groups. Rendering order matches HTML card order. Exactly one group must have kind = meta_gate (the Spec Ambiguity Test).

allOf.description: Exactly one meta_gate group per Quality Bar file (the Spec Ambiguity Test). Enforced via contains + minContains/maxContains on the groups array.

artifact_id_prefix_enum.description: Uppercase prefix used inside per-item IDs. Matches the artifact id prefix convention established by the per-artifact schemas (PB, REQS, ARCH, ADR, DD, TS).

scope_level_enum.description: Scope level in the scope tree — not a scope path. Used by applies_at to tag items that only apply at a specific depth (e.g., TestSpec "Per-layer weight" cases, Architecture "At root scope:" sub-items).

group_kind_enum.description: checklist = ordinary Yes/No group; meta_gate = Spec Ambiguity Test override group. Tools render meta_gate with accent styling and run it after all checklist groups.

trv_rule_id.description: Traceability validation rule id from schemas/traceability/validation-rules.catalog.json. Referenced by Quality Bar items that overlap with a mechanically-enforced rule (e.g., non-empty verifies).

id.description: kebab-case slug of the group title. Used inside item IDs. Must be unique within the file.

title.description: Human-readable group title, verbatim from the HTML <h3>.

load_bearing.description: True when the HTML card carries accent styling (a visually highlighted group that fails louder on review). meta_gate groups must be load_bearing; regular groups may or may not be.

id.description: QB-{ARTIFACT}-{group-slug}-{NNN}. Artifact prefix uppercase from artifact_id_prefix_enum; group slug matches the owning group's id; NNN is 3-digit zero-padded sequence starting at 001, reset per group. IDs are local to the file — not referenced from other artifacts.

prompt.description: The Yes/No review question, plain-text form of the HTML <li>. Passing answer is Yes. Strip any <code> or <em> markup; preserve the substantive wording verbatim.

applies_at.description: Optional. When present, the item only applies at the listed scope level(s). Absence = applies at every layer the artifact type lives at. Used by TestSpec's per-layer weight group and Architecture's root-scope sub-items.

references.description: Optional structured cross-references. Absence = the prompt is purely prose. Only populate when a tool can act on the link — e.g., a field name that a schema validator can cross-check.

fields.description: Names of schema fields this item targets (e.g., "scope_tags", "recovery_status"). Cross-checked against the per-artifact schema at tool-load time, not by this schema.

sections.description: Section markers from the source HTML (e.g., "A", "E", "Composition"). Used by authoring skills to anchor prompts to the artifact's section layout.

rules.description: IDs of overlapping traceability validation rules. Signals that the item is both semantically reviewed here and mechanically checked by the traceability validator — same pattern that validation-rules.catalog.json uses in reverse.
